rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract content, with type scopes for constants and equations, and implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only paths in type contracts.
Validate AGREE scalar types as int, real, or bool; avoid AADL property names as AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee labels with colons, eq for helper declarations, and const for named constants.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or use subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in system implementations
